title: astragalus australis subsp. krajinae
intro: Astragalus australis subsp. krajinae, also known as the Australian milk vetch, is a plant species belonging to the legume family and is native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Astragalus australis subsp. krajinae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its root is used in various herbal preparations to support overall well-being.
